### 1. **Living Room Lighting Function and Energy Consumption**

The function $ N(t) = 300 + 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right) $ represents the natural light in the living room at time $ t $ (in hours after sunrise). The total illumination must be 500 lumens, so the LED light output $ L(t) $ must adjust to maintain this level.

#### Step 1: Determine $ L(t) $

The total illumination is the sum of natural light and LED light:

$$
500 = N(t) + L(t)
$$

Solving for $ L(t) $:

$$
L(t) = 500 - N(t) = 500 - \left(300 + 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)\right)
$$

$$
L(t) = 200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)
$$

#### Step 2: Calculate Total Energy Consumption Over 24 Hours

The LED consumes 0.1 watts per lumen-hour. The total energy consumption is:

$$
\text{Total Energy} = \int_0^{24} L(t) \cdot 0.1 \, dt
$$

Substitute $ L(t) $:

$$
\text{Total Energy} = \int_0^{24} \left(200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)\right) \cdot 0.1 \, dt
$$

$$
= 0.1 \int_0^{24} \left(200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)\right) \, dt
$$

Break the integral into two parts:

$$
= 0.1 \left[ \int_0^{24} 200 \, dt - \int_0^{24} 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right) \, dt \right]
$$

= 0.1 \left[ 200 \cdot 24 - 200 \int_0^{24} \sin\
